subtitle.en: Understanding the celestial stage on which all of Jyotish plays out
subtitle.hi: वह खगोलीय मंच समझें जिस पर सम्पूर्ण ज्योतिष प्रदर्शित होता है

subtitle.kn: ಜ್ಯೋತಿಷ ಸಂಪೂರ್ಣವಾಗಿ ಪ್ರದರ್ಶಿತವಾಗುವ ಖಗೋಳ ವೇದಿಕೆಯನ್ನು ಅರ್ಥಮಾಡಿಕೊಳ್ಳಿ

explanation.en: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.hi: क्रान्तिवृत्त वह मार्ग है जो सूर्य एक वर्ष में तारों की पृष्ठभूमि पर बनाता प्रतीत होता है। यह खगोलीय भूमध्य रेखा से 23.5° झुका हुआ है।
explanation.ta: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.te: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.bn: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.kn: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.mr: क्रान्तिवृत्त वह मार्ग है जो सूर्य एक वर्ष में तारों की पृष्ठभूमि पर बनाता प्रतीत होता है। यह खगोलीय भूमध्य रेखा से 23.5° झुका हुआ है।
explanation.gu: The ecliptic is the apparent path the Sun traces against the background stars over the course of a year. It's a great circle tilted 23.5° to the celestial equator, caused by Earth's axial tilt. In Sanskrit, it's called Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त)  –  the circle of inclination.
explanation.mai: क्रान्तिवृत्त ओ आभासी पथ अछि जे सूर्य एक वर्षक अवधि मे पृष्ठभूमि तारामंडलक विरुद्ध बनाबैत अछि। ई एकटा विशाल वृत्त अछि जे पृथ्वीक अक्षीय झुकावक कारण खगोलीय विषुवत् वृत्त दिस २३.५° झुकल अछि। संस्कृत मे, एकरा क्रान्तिवृत्त (क्रान्तिवृत्त) कहल जाइत अछि – झुकावक वृत्त।

question.en: Why do all planets appear near the ecliptic and never far from it?
question.hi: सभी ग्रह क्रान्तिवृत्त के पास ही क्यों दिखते हैं, कभी दूर नहीं?

question.mr: सभी ग्रह क्रान्तिवृत्त के पास ही क्यों दिखते हैं, कभी दूर नहीं?

question.kn: Why do all planets appear near the ecliptic and never far from it?
question.gu: Why do all planets appear near the ecliptic and never far from it?

explanation.en: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.hi: सौर मंडल एक सपाट गैस और धूल की डिस्क से बना। सभी ग्रह लगभग एक ही तल में कक्षा करते हैं, क्रान्तिवृत्त से अधिकतम 7° विचलन।
explanation.ta: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.te: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.bn: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.kn: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.mr: सौर मंडल एक सपाट गैस और धूल की डिस्क से बना। सभी ग्रह लगभग एक ही तल में कक्षा करते हैं, क्रान्तिवृत्त से अधिकतम 7° विचलन।
explanation.gu: The solar system formed from a flat disk of gas and dust (the solar nebula). All planets orbit in nearly the same plane, deviating at most 7° from the ecliptic. This is why you will NEVER find Jupiter in Ursa Major  –  it's too far from the ecliptic plane.
explanation.mai: सौर मंडल गैस आ धुलिक एकटा सपाट डिस्क (सौर नीहारिका) सँ बनल। सभ ग्रह लगभग एकहि समतल मे परिक्रमा करैत अछि, क्रान्तिवृत्त सँ बेसी सँ बेसी ७° विचलित होइत। एही कारण सँ अहाँ कहियो बृहस्पति केँ सप्तर्षि मंडल मे नहि पाबि सकब – ई क्रान्तिवृत्तक समतल सँ बहुत दूर अछि।

question.mai: पृथ्वीक अक्ष अपन कक्षीय समतलक सापेक्ष केहन कोण पर झुकल अछि?

explanation.en: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.hi: पृथ्वी का अक्षीय झुकाव 23.5° है। यह झुकाव ऋतुओं का कारण है और 25,772 वर्षों में अयनगति (precession) का भी।
explanation.ta: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.te: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.bn: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.kn: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.mr: पृथ्वी का अक्षीय झुकाव 23.5° है। यह झुकाव ऋतुओं का कारण है और 25,772 वर्षों में अयनगति (precession) का भी।
explanation.gu: Earth's axial tilt is 23.5° (also called the obliquity of the ecliptic). This tilt causes the seasons and is the reason the ecliptic is inclined to the celestial equator. It also causes the phenomenon of precession over 25,772 years.
explanation.mai: पृथ्वीक अक्षीय झुकाव २३.५° अछि (एकरा क्रान्तिवृत्तक तिरछापन सेहो कहल जाइत अछि)। ई झुकाव ऋतुसभक कारण बनैत अछि आ एही कारण सँ क्रान्तिवृत्त खगोलीय विषुवत् वृत्त दिस झुकल अछि। ई २५,७७२ वर्षक अवधि मे पुरस्सरणक घटना सेहो उत्पन्न करैत अछि।

question.en: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.
question.hi: क्रान्तिवृत्त और खगोलीय भूमध्य रेखा एक ही वृत्त हैं।
question.mai: क्रान्तिवृत्त आ खगोलीय विषुवत् वृत्त एकहि वृत्त अछि।
question.mr: क्रान्तिवृत्त और खगोलीय भूमध्य रेखा एक ही वृत्त हैं।
question.ta: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.
question.te: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.
question.bn: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.
question.kn: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.
question.gu: The ecliptic and the celestial equator are the same circle.

explanation.en: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.hi: ये दो अलग-अलग वृत्त हैं, एक दूसरे से 23.5° झुके। वे दो बिंदुओं  –  विषुवों (vernal & autumnal equinox)  –  पर मिलते हैं।
explanation.ta: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.te: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.bn: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.kn: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.mr: ये दो अलग-अलग वृत्त हैं, एक दूसरे से 23.5° झुके। वे दो बिंदुओं  –  विषुवों (vernal & autumnal equinox)  –  पर मिलते हैं।
explanation.gu: They are two different great circles on the celestial sphere, tilted 23.5° to each other. They intersect at two points called the equinoxes (Vernal and Autumnal). The ecliptic is the Sun's path; the celestial equator is the projection of Earth's equator.
explanation.mai: ओ सभ खगोलीय गोल पर दूटा भिन्न विशाल वृत्त अछि, जे एक दोसर दिस २३.५° झुकल अछि। ओ सभ दूटा बिंदु पर प्रतिच्छेद करैत अछि जेकरा विषुव (वसंत आ शरद) कहल जाइत अछि। क्रान्तिवृत्त सूर्यक पथ अछि; खगोलीय विषुवत् वृत्त पृथ्वीक विषुवत् वृत्तक प्रक्षेपण अछि।

question.en: The Sanskrit term for the ecliptic is:

explanation.en: Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त) means "the circle of inclination"  –  Kranti = inclination/declination, Vritta = circle. Kha-gola means celestial sphere, Bhagola means the sphere of stars, and Rashi-chakra means the zodiac circle (which lies ON the ecliptic).
explanation.hi: क्रान्तिवृत्त = क्रान्ति (झुकाव) + वृत्त (वृत्त)। खगोल = आकाशीय गोला, भगोल = तारा गोला, राशिचक्र = राशियों का चक्र (जो क्रान्तिवृत्त पर है)।
explanation.mai: क्रान्तिवृत्त (क्रान्तिवृत्त) क अर्थ अछि "झुकावक वृत्त" – क्रान्ति = झुकाव/क्रांति, वृत्त = वृत्त। खगोल क अर्थ खगोलीय गोल, भगोल क अर्थ तारामंडलक गोल, आ राशिचक्र क अर्थ राशिचक्र वृत्त (जे क्रान्तिवृत्त पर रहैत अछि)।
explanation.mr: क्रान्तिवृत्त = क्रान्ति (झुकाव) + वृत्त (वृत्त)। खगोल = आकाशीय गोला, भगोल = तारा गोला, राशिचक्र = राशियों का चक्र (जो क्रान्तिवृत्त पर है)।

explanation.gu: Kranti-vritta (क्रान्तिवृत्त) means "the circle of inclination"  –  Kranti = inclination/declination, Vritta = circle. Kha-gola means celestial sphere, Bhagola means the sphere of stars, and Rashi-chakra means the zodiac circle (which lies ON the ecliptic).

question.en: If someone says "Mars is in Ursa Major tonight," what can you conclude?

explanation.en: This is physically impossible. All planets orbit within ~7° of the ecliptic plane. Ursa Major is near the north celestial pole, roughly 60-90° from the ecliptic. No planet can ever appear there. This is a key test of understanding  –  the ecliptic constrains WHERE planets can be.
explanation.hi: यह भौतिक रूप से असंभव है। सभी ग्रह क्रान्तिवृत्त से ~7° के भीतर कक्षा करते हैं। सप्तर्षि उत्तरी ध्रुव के पास है, क्रान्तिवृत्त से 60-90° दूर।
explanation.mai: ई शारीरिक रूप सँ असंभव अछि। सभ ग्रह क्रान्तिवृत्त समतलक लगभग ७° भीतर परिक्रमा करैत अछि। सप्तर्षि मंडल उत्तरी खगोलीय ध्रुवक नजदीक अछि, क्रान्तिवृत्त सँ लगभग ६०-९०° दूर। कोनो ग्रह कहियो ओतय देखाइ नहि सकैत अछि। ई समझक एकटा मुख्य परीक्षा अछि – क्रान्तिवृत्त ग्रहसभ कतय भऽ सकैत अछि से सीमित करैत अछि।
explanation.mr: यह भौतिक रूप से असंभव है। सभी ग्रह क्रान्तिवृत्त से ~7° के भीतर कक्षा करते हैं। सप्तर्षि उत्तरी ध्रुव के पास है, क्रान्तिवृत्त से 60-90° दूर।

question.en: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.
question.hi: राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्त के दोनों ओर लगभग 8° तक विस्तृत है, 16° चौड़ी पट्टी बनाती है।
question.mai: राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्तक दुनू कात लगभग ८° फैलल अछि, १६° चौड़ा पट्टी बनाबैत।
question.mr: राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्त के दोनों ओर लगभग 8° तक विस्तृत है, 16° चौड़ी पट्टी बनाती है।
question.ta: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.
question.te: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.
question.bn: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.
question.kn: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.
question.gu: The zodiac belt extends about 8° on either side of the ecliptic, forming a 16°-wide band.

explanation.en: Correct. The zodiac belt is approximately 8° on each side of the ecliptic (total ~16° wide). This accommodates the slight orbital inclinations of all planets. The Moon deviates the most at about 5°. The belt is the "highway"  –  only within this zone do planets travel.
explanation.hi: सही। राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्त के दोनों ओर ~8° है (कुल ~16° चौड़ी)। चंद्रमा सबसे अधिक ~5° विचलन करता है।
explanation.mai: सही। राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्तक प्रत्येक कात लगभग ८° अछि (कुल लगभग १६° चौड़ा)। ई सभ ग्रहसभक सामान्य कक्षीय झुकाव केँ समायोजित करैत अछि। चन्द्रमा लगभग ५° पर सबसँ बेसी विचलित होइत अछि। ई पट्टी "राजमार्ग" अछि – मात्र एहि क्षेत्रक भीतर ग्रहसभ यात्रा करैत अछि।
explanation.mr: सही। राशिचक्र पट्टी क्रान्तिवृत्त के दोनों ओर ~8° है (कुल ~16° चौड़ी)। चंद्रमा सबसे अधिक ~5° विचलन करता है।

explanation.en: Earth's 23.5° tilt causes different hemispheres to receive more direct sunlight at different times of year. In Jyotish, this creates the two Ayanas: Uttarayana (Sun moving northward, Jan-Jun) and Dakshinayana (Sun moving southward, Jul-Dec). Interestingly, Earth is actually CLOSEST to the Sun in January (northern winter).
explanation.hi: 23.5° झुकाव के कारण वर्ष के विभिन्न समय में विभिन्न गोलार्ध अधिक सीधी धूप प्राप्त करते हैं। ज्योतिष में यह दो अयन बनाता है: उत्तरायण और दक्षिणायन।
explanation.ta: Earth's 23.5° tilt causes different hemispheres to receive more direct sunlight at different times of year. In Jyotish, this creates the two Ayanas: Uttarayana (Sun moving northward, Jan-Jun) and Dakshinayana (Sun moving southward, Jul-Dec). Interestingly, Earth is actually CLOSEST to the Sun in January (northern winter).

In Jyotish, this creates the two Ayanas: Uttarayana (Sun moving northward, Jan-Jun) and Dakshinayana (Sun moving southward, Jul-Dec). Interestingly, Earth is actually CLOSEST to the Sun in January (northern winter).

In Jyotish, this creates the two Ayanas: Uttarayana (Sun moving northward, Jan-Jun) and Dakshinayana (Sun moving southward, Jul-Dec). Interestingly, Earth is actually CLOSEST to the Sun in January (northern winter).
explanation.mai: पृथ्वीक २३.५° झुकाव क कारण वर्षक भिन्न-भिन्न समय मे भिन्न-भिन्न गोलार्ध बेसी सीधा सूर्यक प्रकाश प्राप्त करैत अछि। ज्योतिष मे, ई दूटा अयन उत्पन्न करैत अछि: उत्तरायण (सूर्य उत्तर दिस जाइत, जनवरी-जून) आ दक्षिणायन (सूर्य दक्षिण दिस जाइत, जुलाई-दिसम्बर)। रोचक बात ई अछि जे, पृथ्वी वास्तव मे जनवरी मे (उत्तरी शीतकाल मे) सूर्यक सबसँ नजदीक रहैत अछि।

explanation.en: By observing which stars were visible just before sunrise and after sunset over many years, ancient astronomers mapped the Sun's annual path through the zodiac. The heliacal risings (stars appearing just before dawn) shift by about 1° per day, tracing the full ecliptic over a year. This required decades of careful observation, not guesswork.
explanation.hi: वर्षों तक सूर्योदय से पहले और सूर्यास्त के बाद कौन से तारे दिखते हैं  –  इसका अवलोकन कर प्राचीन खगोलशास्त्रियों ने सूर्य का वार्षिक मार्ग मानचित्रित किया।
explanation.ta: By observing which stars were visible just before sunrise and after sunset over many years, ancient astronomers mapped the Sun's annual path through the zodiac. The heliacal risings (stars appearing just before dawn) shift by about 1° per day, tracing the full ecliptic over a year. This required decades of careful observation, not guesswork.

explanation.mai: अनेक वर्षसभक दौरान सूर्योदय सँ ठीक पहिने आ सूर्यास्तक बाद कोन-कोन तारा देखाइत छल, से देखि कऽ प्राचीन खगोलशास्त्रीसभ राशिचक्रक माध्यम सँ सूर्यक वार्षिक पथक मानचित्रण केलक। हेलियाकल उदय (भोर सँ ठीक पहिने देखाइ देव बला तारा) प्रतिदिन लगभग १° सँ खसक जाइत अछि, एक वर्षक दौरान पूर्ण क्रान्तिवृत्त केँ चिन्हित करैत। एहि मे दशकसभक सावधानीपूर्वक अवलोकनक आवश्यकता छल, अनुमानक नहि।

question.en: Uttarayana begins when the Sun starts moving northward (around January 14, Makar Sankranti).
question.hi: उत्तरायण तब आरम्भ होता है जब सूर्य उत्तर की ओर बढ़ना शुरू करता है (लगभग 14 जनवरी, मकर संक्रांति)।

explanation.en: Correct. Uttarayana (उत्तर + अयन = northward journey) begins at Makar Sankranti when the Sun enters Capricorn (sidereal). From this point, the Sun moves northward in declination until the summer solstice. Dakshinayana begins ~July 16 when the Sun enters sidereal Cancer. Uttarayana is considered more auspicious  –  Bhishma waited for Uttarayana to leave his body.
explanation.hi: सही। उत्तरायण मकर संक्रांति पर आरम्भ  –  सूर्य उत्तर दिशा में। भीष्म ने शरीर त्यागने के लिए उत्तरायण की प्रतीक्षा की।
explanation.mai: सही। उत्तरायण (उत्तर + अयन = उत्तर दिसक यात्रा) मकर संक्रान्ति सँ शुरू होइत अछि जखन सूर्य मकर राशि मे प्रवेश करैत अछि (निरयण)। एहि बिंदु सँ, सूर्य ग्रीष्म संक्रान्ति धरि क्रांति मे उत्तर दिस बढ़ैत अछि। दक्षिणायन लगभग १६ जुलाई सँ शुरू होइत अछि जखन सूर्य निरयण कर्क राशि मे प्रवेश करैत अछि। उत्तरायण केँ बेसी शुभ मानल जाइत अछि – भीष्म अपन शरीर त्यागबाक लेल उत्तरायणक प्रतीक्षा केलक।
explanation.mr: सही। उत्तरायण मकर संक्रांति पर आरम्भ  –  सूर्य उत्तर दिशा में। भीष्म ने शरीर त्यागने के लिए उत्तरायण की प्रतीक्षा की।
explanation.ta: Correct. Uttarayana (उत्तर + अयन = northward journey) begins at Makar Sankranti when the Sun enters Capricorn (sidereal). From this point, the Sun moves northward in declination until the summer solstice. Dakshinayana begins ~July 16 when the Sun enters sidereal Cancer. Uttarayana is considered more auspicious  –  Bhishma waited for Uttarayana to leave his body.

Uttarayana is considered more auspicious  –  Bhishma waited for Uttarayana to leave his body.
